full_response:
Step 1.  Write down the given quantities  
Proper period of the blinker (Alice’s rest frame)  
 Δτ = 1 s  

Relative speed of Alice with respect to Bob  
 v = 0.8 c  ⇒ β = v/c = 0.8  

Step 2.  Compute the Lorentz factor  
 γ = 1 / √(1 – β²)  
   = 1 / √(1 – 0.8²)  
   = 1 / √(1 – 0.64)  
   = 1 / √0.36  
   = 1 / 0.6  
   = 5/3 ≈ 1.667  

Step 3.  Relate Bob’s coordinate time interval Δt to Alice’s proper time interval Δτ  
For two successive emissions that occur at the same spatial point in Alice’s frame (but different points in Bob’s frame), the interval measured in Bob’s inertial frame is dilated:  
 Δt = γ Δτ  

Insert the numbers:  
 Δt = (5/3) × 1 s = 5/3 s ≈ 1.67 s  

Step 4.  Find the emission frequency according to Bob  
 f_Bob = 1 / Δt = 1 / (5/3 s) = 3/5 Hz = 0.60 Hz  

Thus, in Bob’s frame the blinking device emits a light flash roughly once every 1.67 s.

\boxed{\text{Emission period in Bob's frame } \Delta t = \tfrac{5}{3}\,\text{s} \; \text{(frequency } 0.60\,\text{Hz})}

Confidence: 90%
